axis_d.name: set parity between dispatch (main) and contract (selftest)

fan_out.correlation_method: axis_a (selftest body content) vs axis_b (DRY structure of helper) ~0.40 (helper is part of selftest body); axis_a vs axis_c (non-selftest behavior) ~0.05 (disjoint code paths in main()); axis_a vs axis_d (set parity) ~0.30 (parity is a property of the same body); axis_b vs axis_c ~0.05; axis_b vs axis_d ~0.10; axis_c vs axis_d ~0.10. Pairwise mean ≈ 0.17.

axes_surfaced.name: extension shape — inline 10 copies vs DRY helper

options_considered.label: inline 10 copies of (let r = _check_rawNN(); if len(r) != 3 { eprintln(...); exit(5) }; println(...))
options_considered.rejected_rationale: violates raw 1 transitivity (cargo-cult repetition); ~50 lines vs ~17 with helper; 10 distinct exit-5 sites complicate stack-trace reading

options_considered.label: introduce _assert_shape(label, r) helper; single eprintln + exit(5) routed through it; 10 one-line invocations
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — minimal LOC, single failure path, easy to extend

options_considered.label: iterate over [(label, fn-ref), ...] table-driven
options_considered.rejected_rationale: hexa stdlib does not expose first-class function references in this dialect (no fn-pointer in argv_skip / law_io); table-driven approach would require a meta-eval indirection — over-engineered for 10 entries

options_considered.label: skip selftest extension; fall back to runtime crash semantics in _emit_check_row
options_considered.rejected_rationale: rejected — runtime crash is the failure mode the predecessor cycle's O1 explicitly named; refusing to fix it would leak the obstruction forward

axes_surfaced.rationale: B minimizes LOC and centralizes the failure path. Hexa dialect supports the helper pattern cleanly (string + array param), and the 10 invocations remain diff-readable. Table-driven (C) is reserved for >20 entries.

axes_surfaced.name: scope creep boundary — what is OUT of this cycle

options_considered.label: also wire PreToolUse hook (close O2)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — O2 belongs to a separate harness-wiring cycle (raw 97 follow-up #2); ~/.claude/settings.json edit has cross-session blast radius requiring user confirmation

options_considered.label: also implement audit-ledger cron rotation (close O3)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — launchd plist + state retention policy is independent operational concern

options_considered.label: also escalate delegated-tool absence-skip → fail (close O4)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — semantic escalation requires classifier-version bump (v1 → v2) and corpus-wide impact analysis

options_considered.label: scope strictly to closing O1 (selftest 3/10 → 10/10) and document new sub-residual O5 (set-parity automation)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — minimal-blast cycle; one obstruction in, one obstruction out, one new sub-residual logged

axes_surfaced.rationale: Each obstruction has its own cycle; bundling closes blast radius. This cycle delivers exactly the O1 closure plus an honest declaration of the new O5 (set-parity) sub-residual. Smaller cycles compound faster than mega-cycles per kick(ω-cycle) cadence learnings.

axes_surfaced.name: verification depth — shape-only vs semantic-fixture

options_considered.label: shape-only: assert returned array has 3 elements
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — matches predecessor cycle's O1 closure scope and the existing C1/C2/C10 contract pattern; semantic correctness is verified via production runs (F4)

options_considered.label: shape + verdict-domain: assert verdict ∈ {ok, fail, skip}
options_considered.rejected_rationale: incremental nicety; flagged as future O6 if ever needed

options_considered.label: shape + synthetic-fixture: spawn an in-memory rule fixture per check and assert expected verdict
options_considered.rejected_rationale: much higher implementation cost; better suited to a separate raw-fixture-corpus cycle

options_considered.label: shape + golden-output regression: snapshot stdout JSON and compare on every selftest run
options_considered.rejected_rationale: creates corpus-coupled tests that break on legitimate corpus changes; raw 49 additive-before-destructive friction

axes_surfaced.rationale: A matches the predecessor cycle's contract and the kick topic's named scope (shape coverage). B/C/D belong to a separate verification-deepening cycle.

tier_1_promotions.title: selftest shape coverage RAISED from 3/10 to 10/10 — O1 from stabilize witness CLOSED
tier_1_promotions.claim: _cmd_selftest now emits 10 PASS shape lines (one per dispatched sub-check) and exits 0. Every inline scanner (C3-C9) and every delegated-call wrapper (C1, C2, C10) has a shape contract. Silent regression risk on the 3-element [check-id, exit-code, verdict] tuple is closed for the entire matrix.

tier_1_promotions.title: DRY helper _assert_shape introduces single failure path — raw 1 transitivity preserved
tier_1_promotions.claim: Instead of inlining 10 copies of the if-len-eprintln-exit pattern, a single _assert_shape(label, r) helper centralizes the shape gate. eprintln 'selftest FAIL' count = 1, future C11 extension cost = 1 line. raw 1 maintainability invariant satisfied.

tier_1_promotions.title: Zero regression on production paths — predecessor F5 evidence reproduced
tier_1_promotions.claim: After the edit, hexa tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa --report --target-rule raw:97 still emits fail_count=0, checks_total=10, verdict=ok — bit-identical to predecessor witness F5 evidence. The edit is strictly selftest-local and has no side-effect on the dispatch path.

obstructions.title: set-parity between main dispatch and selftest is enforced manually only
obstructions.evidence: no automated test compares set(main rows.push) against set(selftest _assert_shape calls); a future commit could add a C11 to main() and forget to mirror in selftest
obstructions.impact: drift potential; same class of risk as O1 but at a different layer
obstructions.suggested_close: static analysis pass in selftest itself: parse own source, extract both sets, assert equality. Non-trivial but feasible via _read_file + regex; logged as new sub-cycle.

fixpoint_marker: O1-closure-converged: predecessor obstruction O1 (selftest 3/10) has CLOSED; selftest now exercises 10/10 shape contracts via DRY _assert_shape helper. O2/O3/O4 from predecessor remain open with their own scoped successor cycles. New sub-residual O5 (set-parity automation) logged. NO regression on production paths (F4 PASS).
